why: op6-m14 was appended on 2026-07-19 20:54:41 (commit cfc82852 node + ba086106 parent-child edge) as status:claimed directly, skipping open. Unlike EVERY other op6 node it carries no briefPointer, no discovered-from edge, no plan file, no ledger entry, and no reference anywhere in the repo. The op6 master plan (#263) scopes exactly m1-m7 (barrier-fence + eval-instrument repair, all ssa-lowering/mission-graph/suite-format/case-judge/persona-voice). m8-m13 are discovery-time additions, each fully provenanced with a discovered-from edge and a GitHub issue briefPointer (#304/#305/#306/#308/#319). m14 alone has neither, and its touch-set [sdd/authoring, sdd/design, sdd/workflows] is outside op6's theme entirely. Contrast the same-evening m13 batch (17:33:41): node WITH briefPointer #304 + parent-child + discovered-from(op6-m10). m14's 20:54:41 batch: node with NO briefPointer + parent-child ONLY. It is a naked placeholder stub that never became a defined mission; the stale claim released today had no live claimant and no brief to run. No mission was invented to justify it (per brief). Retiring so op6 closes 14/14. Reversible: append a node event to reopen if the owner intends real authoring/design/workflows work — which, if real, deserves its own properly-briefed node/operation, not this orphan.
